summary: Moved Peach down-special / vegetable pull code out of the monolithic Peach init assembly into a new `ftPe_SpecialLw.c`/`.h` unit, partially decompiling the state enter/anim/phys/coll logic while keeping hard-to-match pieces guarded with `MUST_MATCH` inline assembly. The PR also improved supporting type/header knowledge: renamed the generic held-item fighter field to `item_gobj`, identified Peach's `fp+2248` as `veg_gobj`, exposed item spawn/delete prototypes, added Peach special headers, and registered the new object file. There were no review comments in the slice.

key_files.role: new partial C decompilation for Peach SpecialLw/SpecialAirLw, vegetable selection/spawn/delete helpers, callbacks, motion-state transitions, physics, and collision handlers
new public declarations for Peach down-special functions at 11CF84 through 11D3FC
removed the corresponding Peach down-special assembly block from the init asm file
renamed Peach fighter var `x20` to `veg_gobj` and split early `ftPe_DatAttrs` bytes into fields used by vegetable selection
updated held-item inline helpers to use `item_gobj` and added `ftCheckThrowB0` helper for throw flag consumption
added `FtPart_109 = 109`, used as the vegetable spawn attachment point
added prototypes for `it_802BD4AC` and `it_802BD45C`, used by Peach vegetable spawn/delete
added `ftPe_SpecialLw.c.o` before the remaining Peach init assembly object

what_changed: Added `ftPe_SpecialLw.c` with partial C implementations for Peach down-special, including `ftPe_SpecialLw_Enter`, `ftPe_SpecialAirLw_Enter`, animation callbacks, ground/air physics, ground/air collision transitions, `throwVegIfHeld`, `getVeg`, `spawnVeg`, `pickVeg`, and cleanup helpers.
Kept `pickVeg` and `spawnVeg` under `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` inline assembly, with non-asm C attempts available for WIP/non-MUST_MATCH builds.

Removed the matching assembly range from `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPe_Init.s`, including symbols such as `ftPe_8011CE48`, `ftPe_8011CF84`, `ftPe_8011CFA0`, `ftPe_SpecialLw_Enter`, `ftPe_SpecialAirLw_Enter`, anim/phys/coll routines, and related local labels.
Added `ftPe_SpecialLw.h`, plus new Peach `ftPe_SpecialHi.h` and `ftPe_SpecialN.h` headers, and moved Peach special declarations out of `ftPe_Init.h`.
Integrated the new Peach SpecialLw object into `obj_files.mk`.

Renamed the global fighter held item pointer at `fp+1974` from `x1974_heldItem` to `item_gobj` and propagated that rename through common fighter code, ftlib, Peach, Purin, Game & Watch, Link/Young Link, Mewtwo, Samus, and other touched call sites.
Renamed Peach fighter variable `fp+2248` from `x20` to `veg_gobj`, reset it on Peach death, and used it to track the vegetable object separate from the generic held-item field.
Exposed item functions `it_802BD4AC` and `it_802BD45C` in `it_27CF.h`; these are used to create and remove Peach turnip/vegetable items.
Added `FtPart_109 = 109` to `Fighter_Part`, matching the joint/part used to compute the vegetable spawn position via `lb_8000B1CC`.
smart_moves: Split Peach down-special into its own translation unit instead of leaving it embedded in `ftPe_Init.s`, which makes the motion-state logic searchable and isolates remaining nonmatching assembly.
Used `MUST_MATCH && !WIP` inline assembly only for the hardest routines, allowing partial C progress without sacrificing matching builds.

Introduced small semantic helpers (`getVeg`, `throwVegIfHeld`, `doAnim`, `handleAirColl`, `handleColl`) around repeated behavior, which clarified the state machine while preserving the original control flow at the exported function level.
Renaming `x1974_heldItem` to `item_gobj` across many files gave future decomp work a reliable shared name for the fighter held-item pointer and reduced dependence on raw offsets.
Identifying `fp->fv.pe.veg_gobj` captured the distinction between Peach's tracked vegetable object and the generic `item_gobj`, which is important for cleanup and turnip-specific behavior.

The code preserved fragile matching details explicitly, including the `MUST_MATCH` unused stack note in `ftPe_SpecialLw_Anim`, the static `coll_mf` bitmask, and direct callback assignments to `x21BC`, `x21E4`, and `x21DC`.
decomp_lessons: When only part of a function cluster is matchable, moving the cluster to C with local `MUST_MATCH` asm islands can still retire large sections of standalone asm and improve code navigation.
Field renames should be propagated broadly once the semantics are clear; `item_gobj` became useful across fighter common code, character-specific item callbacks, and ftlib helpers.

Character-specific fighter vars often shadow or track global fighter state; Peach's `veg_gobj` must be distinguished from `item_gobj` because cleanup checks compare both and verify `It_Kind_Peach_Turnip`.
Motion-state decompilation benefits from factoring shared animation-end behavior: both ground and air down-special call an end-state callback then unset the tracked vegetable.

Header extraction is part of matching work: adding focused Peach special headers allowed `ftdata.c` and `ftPe_Init.c` to reference declarations without keeping all special-move prototypes in `ftPe_Init.h`.

Dat attribute structs can be refined incrementally; this PR split `ftPe_DatAttrs` at offsets `+10`, `+14`, and `+18` because vegetable RNG/weighting code accessed those fields directly, while leaving later unknown bytes as padding.
naming_conventions: `Fighter::x1974_heldItem` was renamed to `Fighter::item_gobj` for the generic held item `HSD_GObj*`.
`ftPeach_FighterVars::x20` at `fp+2248` was renamed to `veg_gobj` for Peach's tracked vegetable object.
`veg` was documented in the glossary as short for vegetable.
New Peach down-special functions follow existing exported naming: `ftPe_SpecialLw_*` for grounded versions and `ftPe_SpecialAirLw_*` for aerial versions.
Internal helper names use behavior-oriented verbs: `pickVeg`, `spawnVeg`, `getVeg`, `throwVegIfHeld`, `handleAirColl`, `handleColl`, and `doAnim`.
New headers used address-style include guards such as `GALE01_11D11C`, consistent with the function address range represented by the header.
Item kind comparisons used named constants where available, notably `It_Kind_Peach_Turnip`, `It_Kind_Parasol`, `It_Kind_Peach_Parasol`, and `It_Kind_Sword`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed a large Peach down-special assembly block from `ftPe_Init.s` while preserving exact asm for `pickVeg` and `spawnVeg` inside the new C file under `MUST_MATCH && !WIP`.
Used `#pragma push`/`#pragma pop`, `static asm`, `nofralloc`, and `// clang-format off` around inline assembly to keep compiler formatting and prologue behavior stable.
Kept local labels in inline asm as `lbl_8011....` equivalents of the removed `.L_...` labels.
Provided non-asm C attempts for `pickVeg` and `spawnVeg` in the `#else` path, including `M2C_UNK` and `M2C_FIELD` for unresolved pointer arithmetic in the weighted vegetable selection loop.
Preserved a matching workaround in `ftPe_SpecialLw_Anim` with a `MUST_MATCH` unused stack byte array.

Used a `static MotionFlags const coll_mf` expression to encode the shared ground/air transition flags (`Ft_MF_SkipMatAnim`, `Ft_MF_SkipColAnim`, `Ft_MF_UpdateCmd`, `Ft_MF_SkipItemVis`, `Ft_MF_Unk19`, `Ft_MF_SkipModelPartVis`, `Ft_MF_SkipModelFlags`, `Ft_MF_Unk27`).
Factored animation completion into `doAnim(gobj, cb)` so ground and air animation functions share the same `ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining` pattern while passing `ft_8008A2BC` or `ft_800CC730`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match `pickVeg` or remove the `M2C_UNK`/`M2C_FIELD` code in `ftPe_SpecialLw.c`.
Search for later renames of `ftPe_SpecialLw_8011CFA0` and `ftPe_SpecialLw_UnsetVeg`.
Search for follow-up struct work on `ftPe_DatAttrs` offsets `+10`, `+14`, `+18`, `+1C`, and turnip probability tables.
Search for later cleanup of `FtPart_109` into a semantic part name.
Search for broader adoption or corrections of the `item_gobj` held-item field rename.
